analysis.device: NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition

bottleneck.description: Every GEMM dispatches to the CUTLASS SIMT FP32 path (cutlass_80_simt_sgemm_128x256_8x4_tn_align1) which runs entirely on CUDA cores: tensor_core_active_pct = 0.0 and smsp__pipe_tensor_cycles_active = 0 on all 8 mm kernels. The model executes in float32, so cuBLAS/CUTLASS never selects a tensor-core HGEMM. SM throughput sits at ~36% with DRAM at only ~7.5%, so the kernels are compute-path-limited on the SIMT pipe, not memory-bound -- the dedicated Blackwell tensor-core matrix units are 100% idle. The two SDPA ops dispatch to fmha_cutlassF_f32_aligned_64x64_rf_sm80, the float32 mem-efficient attention kernel built for the sm80 (Ampere) arch -- also a non-tensor-core, legacy-arch path on this Blackwell GPU; together the GEMMs + SDPA are ~97% of attributed time.

transformation.description: Promote the attention block's activations and weights to bfloat16 (autocast region or explicit cast pass over the decomposed graph). This re-routes every aten.mm from the SIMT FP32 cutlass_80_simt_sgemm path to a tensor-core HGEMM, and re-routes SDPA from the f32 sm80 mem-efficient kernel to the bf16 FlashAttention tensor-core path (mem_efficient/flash f16 fmha). bf16 keeps the dynamic range of fp32 (8-bit exponent), so for inference of LayerNorm + attention it is the low-risk choice over fp16. Promotion also halves the DRAM bytes the memory-bound LayerNorm/view kernels move.
transformation.location: decomposed aten graph (post-AOTAutograd); insert dtype casts in front of each aten.mm input and on attention q/k/v, or wrap forward in torch.autocast(device_type='cuda', dtype=torch.bfloat16)
fx_steps: # aten-level dtype promotion pass over the AOT graph_module
if node.op == 'call_function' and node.target in mm_overloads:
# simplest equivalent at the model level:
transformation.code_hint: Prefer torch.autocast(device_type='cuda', dtype=torch.bfloat16) wrapping the forward, OR keep the model in bf16 (model.to(torch.bfloat16)); under torch.compile Inductor will then lower aten.mm to tensor-core HGEMM and SDPA to the bf16 flash/mem-efficient path. Validate attention output max-abs error stays within tolerance.

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: GEMM weight/activation reads halved (fp32->bf16); LayerNorm/view DRAM traffic halved. Primary win is compute-path: SIMT FP32 -> tensor-core HGEMM and f32-sm80 mem-efficient attention -> bf16 flash.

optimizations.notes: ncu duration_ns is 2-5x inflated and used only for relative ranking. Tensor-core engagement is directly evidenced by the 0.0 tensor_core_active_pct and the literal 'simt_sgemm' / 'f32...sm80' kernel names. SDPA op metrics are empty ('raw': {}) because the op carries is_fused=true (fused_kernel_double_count flag) -- diagnosis there rests on the kernel name, not counters. Estimate is conservative; tensor-core HGEMM on Blackwell is typically 2-4x the SIMT FP32 path for these compute-bound shapes.

operators: aten::mm (op_id=4 q_proj, op_id=5 k_proj, op_id=6 v_proj) (3 nodes per pass)

bottleneck.description: q_proj, k_proj, v_proj are three independent [4096,512] x [512,512] GEMMs that all consume the SAME ln_pre activation (their NVTX input_op_ids share source (0,-1)). Inductor did NOT fuse them: they appear as three separate cutlass kernels (k_00259/260/261) launched back-to-back. Each launches a grid of [64,1,2] = 128 thread blocks against a 188-SM device, leaving ~60 SMs idle (Waves/SM = ceil(128/188) = 1 partially-filled wave) and running at only 16.6% achieved occupancy. Three serial small launches pay 3x launch + tail overhead and under-fill the GPU.

transformation.description: Fuse q_proj/k_proj/v_proj into a single F.linear with a concatenated [1536,512] weight, then split the [4096,1536] output into three [4096,512] tensors before the view/transpose into heads. This replaces 3 GEMMs with 1 wide GEMM per forward pass. The wider N=1536 produces grid [64,1,6] = 384 blocks (~2 full waves on 188 SMs) instead of 128, raising SM utilization and eliminating 2 kernel launches per pass (4 across the 2 captured passes). Must be expressed at the functional level: the three projections share ONE activation node here, but after AOTAutograd decomposition each consumes its own aten.view and (after OPT-1) its own cast, so the shared-activation identity the matcher needs is gone at the aten level.
transformation.location: Dynamo/functional graph, before decomposition: replace the three nn.Linear/F.linear projection nodes feeding the same ln_pre output with one fused F.linear + split
fx_steps: # build-time: concatenate the three projection weights into one buffer
# functional-graph surgery sketch (matcher keys on shared activation 'x'):
#   find call_module/call_function nodes {q_proj, k_proj, v_proj} whose first arg is the same Node
#   insert a single fused linear node: fused = g.call_module('qkv', (x,))
#   q,k,v = split(fused, 512, dim=-1)  via torch.ops.aten.split or three slice nodes
#   replace_all_uses_with: q_node->q, k_node->k, v_node->v ; erase the three originals
q, k, v = fused.split(512, dim=-1)   # three [B*T, 512] views
transformation.code_hint: Concatenate weights at module-construction time and register one nn.Linear(512, 1536, bias=False); torch.compile will lower it to a single aten.mm with N=1536. Keep the .view(B,T,NUM_HEADS,HEAD_DIM).transpose(1,2) per split output unchanged.

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: ln_pre activation read once instead of 3x; better L2 reuse of the shared input. Wider GEMM doubles wave occupancy (128 -> 384 blocks).

optimizations.notes: Runs at the functional level, so it executes BEFORE OPT-1 (aten) in the backend funnel (functional -> aten -> inductor_config). The fused [1536,512] weight buffer is therefore allocated first and then cast to bf16 by OPT-1 at the runtime dtype -- satisfying the 'register_buffer after dtype promotion' rule by level ordering, with no explicit cross-level prerequisite edge (which would be unsatisfiable). Impact is incremental on top of OPT-1; combining both gives one wide tensor-core HGEMM.

bottleneck.description: The projection weights are eval()-mode constants but Inductor re-reads them as live tensors each call and cannot pre-transpose/pre-pack them. The simt_sgemm '_tn_' kernel implies a transposing load of the weight (B operand) on every invocation; with freezing enabled Inductor can constant-fold the weight layout (pre-transpose / pre-pack to the GEMM's preferred layout) once at compile time. This is a layout/lowering decision Inductor already owns, not a graph rewrite. Lower-confidence, smaller win, and complementary to OPT-1/OPT-2.

transformation.description: Enable Inductor weight freezing so constant eval-mode projection weights are folded and pre-laid-out for the (post-OPT-1) tensor-core HGEMM, removing per-call transpose/pack work. Apply after dtype promotion so the frozen constant is stored at the runtime bf16 dtype (freezing materializes a constant buffer, which the general rule requires to follow dtype promotion).
transformation.location: Inductor compile config (config_patches), applied at the inductor_config stage of the funnel
fx_steps: cfg.freezing = True            # fold + pre-layout constant weights
# requires inference (no_grad / eval) so weights are treated as constants
transformation.code_hint: torch.compile(model, options={'freezing': True}) or set torch._inductor.config.freezing = True before compile; only valid for inference (eval mode), which this workload uses (.eval()).

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: Eliminates per-call weight transpose/repack; improves weight-load locality (L1 sector hit rate currently 10.8%).

optimizations.notes: inductor_config stage runs last in the funnel (after functional OPT-2 and aten OPT-1), so the frozen constant is naturally bf16 and reflects the fused QKV weight -- ordering is satisfied by level with no explicit edge. Smallest of the three wins; recommend as a cheap add-on once OPT-1/OPT-2 land. Estimate is conservative and relative only (ncu duration_ns is 2-5x inflated).

global_notes: 0 operators below the 1% threshold omitted (combined 0% of attributed time) -- all 12 measured operators are >= 1%.

Device 'NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition' is not present in any knowledge/hardware-limits.md (the knowledge/ directory does not exist in this repo). Assumed Blackwell GB202-class limits: ~188 SMs, and per the Blackwell rule warp_cycles_per_instruction is unavailable (confirmed: all aggregated.warp_cycles_per_instruction are null) so eligible_cycles_pct (~3.6-4.7% on memory-bound LayerNorm/view, ~58% on GEMMs) was used as the latency-bound indicator. Waves/SM and SM-idle estimates depend on the assumed 188 SM count; treat the wave-quantization numbers as approximate.

MCP tools (context7, exa-search, memory, sequential-thinking) were unavailable; FX API patterns drawn from built-in knowledge per the documented fallback. Novel-transform confidence cap not triggered -- OPT-1/OPT-2 are standard, well-established transforms.

edge_case_flag 'fused_kernel_double_count': both aten::_efficient_attention_forward ops carry is_fused=true and fused_with the SDPA op; their ncu metric blocks are empty ('raw': {}). SDPA diagnosis therefore relies on the kernel name (fmha_cutlassF_f32_aligned_..._sm80) rather than counters, and their durations should not be summed with the un-fused SDPA op to avoid double counting.

warm_up_inflation could not be evaluated: capture_metadata has no measure_iters field. The profile contains two near-identical sets of op_ids (4/5/6/21 and 26/27/28/43) plus two SDPA ops, consistent with 2 measured forward passes; per-op kernel_count is 1 for each mm. If measure_iters differs from the per-op kernel multiplicity, attribution indexing assumptions in the spec would warrant the flag -- not raised here due to missing metadata.

All measured kernels are on a single stream (stream_id=7): no multi_stream_overlap. unattributed_kernels is empty (high_unattributed not triggered); the 258 entries in 'warnings' are pre-NVTX warm-up/init kernels correctly excluded from the budget.

Combined expected effect of OPT-1 + OPT-2 + OPT-3: the ~97%-of-time FP32 SIMT GEMM + f32-sm80 attention workload converts to fused bf16 tensor-core HGEMM + bf16 FlashAttention with pre-laid-out frozen weights -- the dominant lever is OPT-1 (dtype promotion to engage the idle tensor cores).
